Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real technique behind it. Marcus confirmed the cornmeal-jalapeño structure was legitimate. Stephanie proposed leading with the moisture-seal angle, which Margaret approved - Marcus will anchor copy around how the cornmeal crust concentrates heat on the bite.

Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should anchor on crust performance rather than ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three bite-through shots. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - saying "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?" Margaret agreed, prioritizing the crisp over the jalapeno cross-section Julian had championed. The team locked in shot two.

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focused on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic over taste. Stephanie said "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" then asked Marcus to cut repetition. Margaret approved the tightened version - lead with cornmeal arresting moisture, show the fry result, skip the explanation.

Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - expecting problems. Marcus pushed back, saying trusting the work improved it. Stephanie credited the team for listening over defending positions. Marcus and Julian both agreed restraint - in copy and camera angles - was the key decision that made the project land.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - no ingredients, techniques, or food ideas were mentioned beyond a passing metaphor. Devon handled a technical deployment, confirming verification passed and pushing live. Margaret advocated for restraint as a guiding principle. Still open: what the actual recipe discussion was meant to cover.
